questions.stem: For a gear-train driven DC motor system with gear ratio 1/2 and given motor inertia J4=32 kg-mآ² and other parameters, compute the equivalent inertia (Jeq) and equivalent damping (Deq) referred to the motor shaft.
common_errors: Forgetting to square the gear ratio when reflecting inertia/damping (Nآ²آ·J rule)
Missing the +1 additive term for motor rotor inertia
Using incorrect gear ratio (n=1/2 vs n=2)
questions.solution_method: Apply reflected inertia formula Jeq = (N1/N2)آ²آ·J_load + J_motor for each stage; apply same squaring rule to damping Deq = (N1/N2)آ²آ·B_load. Substitute numerical values: Jeq = (1/16)أ—32 + (1/4)أ—4 + 1 = 4 kg-mآ²; Deq = (1/3)(1/2)آ²أ—16 = 4/3 N-m-s/rad.

questions.stem: Draw the block diagram of the DC motor system (with gear train, gear ratio 1/2) relating armature voltage Ea(s) to motor shaft angle خ¸m(s). Given the torque-speed characteristics showing rated torque 5 N-m at 0 RPM and zero torque at 600/د€ RPM (â‰ˆ20 rad/s), determine Kt/Ra and Ktآ·Kb/Ra. Hence derive the transfer function خ¸m(s)/Ea(s).
common_errors: Incorrect identification of Kt/Ra from stall torque (intercept at د‰=0, not slope)
Sign error in back-EMF term Eb = Kbآ·د‰m in the armature circuit
Not applying gear ratio correctly when relating خ¸m to خ¸L in the block diagram
Missing factor of 1/s to convert د‰m(s) to خ¸m(s)
questions.solution_method: From torque-speed characteristic: Kt/Ra = T_stall/Ea_rated = 5/5 = 1; slope gives Ktآ·Kb/Ra = (5-0)/(20-0) = 1/4. Draw block diagram with forward path 1/Ra â†’ Kt â†’ 1/(Jeqآ·s + Beq) â†’ 1/s and back-EMF feedback Kb. Derive closed-loop TF: خ¸m(s)/Ea(s) = (1/8)آ·[0.8/(3.2s+1)] after substituting Jeq=4, Beq=(Beq+KtKb/Ra)=1+1/4=5/4.

questions.stem: Given the gear ratio between load shaft and motor shaft is 1/4 (خ¸L/خ¸m = 1/4), find the transfer function خ¸L(s)/Ea(s).
common_errors: Applying gear ratio incorrectly (multiplying by 4 instead of 1/4)
Using wrong TF from part (b) as base
questions.solution_method: Multiply خ¸m(s)/Ea(s) by gear ratio خ¸L/خ¸m = 1/4 to get خ¸L(s)/Ea(s) = (0.25/8)آ·[0.8/(3.2s+1)] = (0.025)آ·[1/(0.3194s+1)].

questions.stem: A rotational system drives a translational system through a rack-and-pinion (radius r=2 m, gear ratio 2:1 motor-to-load). Motor inertia Jm=1 kg-mآ², load inertia JL=1 kg-mآ², load damping BL=1 N-m-s/rad, spring constant K N/m, translational mass m kg, translational damping B=8 N-s/m. (a) Derive the transfer function X(s)/Tm(s). (b) For settling time ts=15 s, determine the mass m. (Use 2% criterion.)
common_errors: Not reflecting translational-side forces (F=msآ²+Bs) as equivalent torque via Fآ·r
Incorrect gear ratio application when combining Jeq on load side vs motor side
Error in computing Jeq at load side: forgetting (N2/N1)آ²آ·Jm contribution
Wrong settling-time formula: ts=4/(خ¶آ·د‰n) for 2% criterion, not ts=4/د‰n
Algebraic error in solving for m from ts constraint
questions.solution_method: Refer all inertia to load shaft: Jeq=1+(2)آ²أ—1=5 kg-mآ², Beq=0+(2)آ²أ—1=4 N-m-s/rad. Write torque balance at load gear: Teqآ·خ¸Lجˆ+Beqآ·خ¸Lج‡+Kآ·خ¸L = Tm_reflected + F(s)آ·r where F(s)=(msآ²+Bs)آ·X(s) and X(s)=rآ·خ¸L(s). Substitute r=2 and gear ratio (Tm_reflected=2Tm). Derive X(s)/Tm(s) = 4/[(5+4m)sآ²+8s+K]. For ts=15: 4/[4B/(5+4m)]=15 â†’ m=10/4=2.5 kg.

questions.stem: A unity-feedback control system has open-loop transfer function G(s) = K/[(s+100)(s+20)آ²]. Determine the range of K for closed-loop stability using the Routh-Hurwitz criterion.
common_errors: Arithmetic error in expanding (s+100)(s+20)آ²
Incorrect Routh array construction (wrong pivot element computation)
Forgetting both conditions: sâپ° row > 0 AND sآ¹ row element > 0
Not stating both bounds: missing the lower bound K > -4000
questions.solution_method: Form closed-loop CE: 1+G(s)=0 â†’ sآ³+140sآ²+4400s+(40000+10K)=0. Construct 4-row Routh array. sآ³: [1, 4400]; sآ²: [140, 40000+10K]; sآ¹: [خ±=(140أ—4400âˆ’(40000+10K))/140]; sâپ°: [40000+10K]. Stability requires خ±>0 and 40000+10K>0 â†’ K<57,600 and K>âˆ’4000.
key_formulas: Stability: all Routh column-1 elements positive

questions.stem: For G(s) = K/[(s+100)(s+20)آ²] in unity feedback, find the value of K such that the closed-loop system has a damping ratio خ¶=0.69 (i.e., approximately 5% overshoot). Identify the closed-loop poles and verify the dominance of complex poles.
common_errors: Using Mp=e^(-د€خ¶/âˆڑ(1-خ¶آ²)) and reading خ¶=0.69 for ~5% (correct) but miscalculating
Sign error when solving for 'a' (real pole location) from coefficient comparison
Selecting the unstable root د‰n=187 instead of valid د‰n=25.92
Not checking that the real pole sâ‚پâ‰ˆ6أ—(خ¶د‰n) to confirm it is non-dominant
questions.solution_method: Assume CE factors as (s+a)(sآ²+2خ¶د‰nآ·s+د‰nآ²)=0 with خ¶=0.69. Expand and match coefficients against sآ³+140sآ²+4400s+(40000+10K): get three equations. From (1.38د‰n+a)=140 and (د‰nآ²+1.38aآ·د‰n)=4400, solve to get د‰n=25.92 (د‰n=187 rejected as it makes a<0). Then a=104.23, Kâ‰ˆ3002. Complex poles sâ‚‚,â‚ƒ=âˆ’17.385آ±j18.76; real pole sâ‚پ=âˆ’104.23â‰ˆ6(خ¶د‰n), confirming 5% overshoot approximation valid.

questions.stem: A relay-controlled first-order system has G(s)=2/(10s+1). The relay output u(t) switches between 0.8 and 0 based on error thresholds: relay turns ON (u=0.8) when e(t)>0.2, turns OFF (u=0) when e(t)<âˆ’0.2 or c(t)>0.8. With unit step reference (r=1), find the switching times tâ‚€ (first switch OFF) and tâ‚پ (first switch back ON) and tâ‚‚ (second switch OFF).
common_errors: Incorrect initial/final values in first-order step response formula c(t)=c_final+(c_initialâˆ’c_final)e^(âˆ’t/د„)
Not tracking the correct switching condition (c>1.2 vs c>0.8)
Using wrong cfinal when relay switches to 0 (cfinal=0, not 0.8)
Arithmetic error in computing tâ‚پ' and adding to tâ‚€ to get tâ‚پ
questions.solution_method: System gain=2, د„=10 s. Phase 1 (u=0.8): c_final=1.6, c_initial=0; switch OFF when c(tâ‚€)=1.2: 1.2=1.6(1âˆ’e^(âˆ’tâ‚€/10)) â†’ tâ‚€=13.863 s. Phase 2 (u=0): c_final=0, c_initial=1.2; switch ON when c drops to 0.8: 0.8=1.2آ·e^(âˆ’tâ‚پ'/10) â†’ tâ‚پ'=4.054 s, so tâ‚پ=17.917 s. Phase 3 (u=0.8): c_final=1.6, c_initial=0.8; switch OFF when c(tâ‚‚)=1.2: 1.2=1.6+(0.8âˆ’1.6)e^(âˆ’tâ‚‚'/10) â†’ tâ‚‚'=6.931 s, tâ‚‚=28.85 s.

questions.stem: For the relay-controlled system in Q4(a), determine the period of oscillation of the relay switching cycle.
common_errors: Using total time span instead of one ON+OFF half-cycle sum
Not summing tâ‚پ' and tâ‚‚' correctly (4.054+6.931)
questions.solution_method: Period = duration of one complete ON-OFF cycle = tâ‚پ' + tâ‚‚' = 4.054 + 6.931 = 10.985 s.

questions.stem: Sketch the relay control signal u(t) and output c(t) vs time for the relay-controlled system in Q4, marking the switching instants t=0, 13.863, 17.917, 28.85 s.
common_errors: Incorrect timing of switching instants on the sketch
Not showing u(t) switching between 0 and 0.8 (confusing with آ±1 relay)
questions.solution_method: Draw u(t) as rectangular wave: HIGH (0.8) from 0â†’13.863, LOW (0) from 13.863â†’17.917, HIGH again 17.917â†’28.85. Show c(t) rising toward 1.6, hitting 1.2 at 13.863, falling to 0.8 at 17.917, rising again to 1.2 at 28.85.

notes: Handwritten solution with circled marks per sub-part. Q1 is DC motor/gear-train modelling (3 sub-parts, 15 marks total). Q2 is rotational-translational coupled system (13 marks). Q3 is RH stability + pole placement for خ¶ (two sub-parts, 15 marks). Q4 is relay-control first-order system (three sub-parts, 15 marks). No question paper present â€” all stems reconstructed from solution layout. Marking scheme annotations (circled numbers) used to assign marks per sub-part.
